zh.lifeSummary: Richard Chamberlain是William Chamberlain和Joan的兒子，於大約1450年出生在英國奧可福爾爾本。他和Sybil Fowler至少有9個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1496年8月28日在英國英格蘭奧可福爾爾本享年47歲，埋葬在英國奧可福爾爾本。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Chamberlain是William Chamberlain和Joan的儿子，于大约1450年出生在英国奧可福爾爾本。他和Sybil Fowler至少有9个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1496年8月28日在英国英格兰奧可福爾爾本享年47岁，埋葬在英国奧可福爾爾本。

nameMeanings.name: Chamberlain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name from Old French chambrelain, Norman French cambrelanc, cambrelen(c) ‘chamberlain’ (of ancient Germanic origin, from kamer ‘chamber, room’, Latin camera (see Chambers ) + the diminutive suffix -(l)ing). This was originally the name of an official in charge of the private chambers of his master, but is so widespread in late medieval England that it must sometimes have been used of people of more ordinary status, perhaps as a nickname for an officious or self-important person or for someone who played the role of chamberlain in a folk play, tableau, or ceremony. Compare Chancellor for a possible similar usage.

variants.name: Chamberlin
meanings: French: occupational name for an official in charge of the private chambers of his master, from Old French chamberlenc ‘chamberlain’. See also Chamberlain and compare above.

variants.name: Chambers
meanings: from Middle English chaumbre ‘room (in a house); reception room in a palace or official building’ (Old French chambre). It is identical in implied function with the Chamberlain , which denoted an official: to pay in cameram was to pay into the exchequer, of which the camerarius or chamberer was in charge. The surname also applied to clerks employed there. As the office of Chamberlain rose in the social scale, this term remained reserved for more humble servants of the bedchamber or private quarters.
(of Norman origin): habitational name from Les Chambres, Manche (France).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Chamberlain was born about 1450, in Shirburn, Oxfordshire, England as the son of William Chamberlain and Joan. He had at least 9 sons and 1 daughter with Sybil Fowler. He died on 28 August 1496, in Shirburn, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 47, and was buried in Shirburn, Oxfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Chamberlain est né environ 1450, à Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, fils de William Chamberlain et Joan. Il a eu au moins 9 fils et 1 fille avec Sybil Fowler. Il est décédé le 28 août 1496, à Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Chamberlain wurde um 1450 in Shirburn, Oxfordshire, England, als Sohn von William Chamberlain und Joan geboren. Er hatte mit Sybil Fowler mindestens 9 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 28. August 1496 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Shirburn, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Shirburn, Oxfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Chamberlain è nato il circa 1450 nel Shirburn, Oxfordshire, England come figlio di William Chamberlain e Joan. Lui avuto almeno 9 figli e 1 figlia con Sybil Fowler. Morì il 28 agosto 1496, nel Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolto a Shirburn, Oxfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Chamberlainは約1450年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアシュイルブルンでWilliam ChamberlainとJoanの息子として生まれた。彼はSybil Fowlerとの間に少なくとも9人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1496年8月28日にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアシュイルブルンで47歳で死去し，イギリスオクスフォードシュアイアシュイルブルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Chamberlain(은)는 대략 1450년 영국 스퍼드실르 실르번에서 William Chamberlain(와)과 Joan의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Sybil Fowler(와)과 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1496년 8월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 실르번에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스퍼드실르 실르번에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Chamberlain nasceu aproximadamente 1450, em Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra filho de William Chamberlain e Joan. Ele teve pelo menos 9 filhos e 1 filha com Sybil Fowler. Ele faleceu em 28 de agosto de 1496, em Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 47 anos, e foi sepultado em Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Chamberlain родился около 1450 г. в местности ширбурн, оксфордшир, Англия. Его родителей звали William Chamberlain и Joan. У него было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 1 дочь от Sybil Fowler. Он умер 28 августа 1496 г. в местности ширбурн, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности ширбурн, оксфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Chamberlain nació aproximadamente 1450, en Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra como hijo de William Chamberlain y Joan. Tuvo por lo menos 9 hijos y 1 hija con Sybil Fowler. Murió el 28 de agosto de 1496, en Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultado en Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra.
